message:
feat(search): land truthful readiness vocabulary (bead ibuuh.9)

First slice of bead ibuuh.9: introduce the orthogonal readiness
vocabulary that future status / health / capabilities / search
metadata payloads will project into their JSON. The wiring into the
actual payload builders in `src/lib.rs` and into the TUI status
surface ships in follow-up slices; this commit lands vocabulary only.

Motivation: cass currently reports a single `healthy / unhealthy`
bit that conflates five genuinely distinct states — "lexical index
missing" (broken; search returns nothing), "lexical index stale
but searchable" (slightly old, fully correct), "lexical index
rebuilding" (search works; new docs lag), "lexical corrupt /
quarantined" (broken with forensic hold), and "semantic tier still
backfilling" (lexical is correct; hybrid refinement catches up
later). That conflation drives unnecessary repair rituals because
agents cannot distinguish the cases from the JSON payload, so they
default to the nuclear option every time they see `healthy=false`.

- Register `src/search/readiness` as `pub(crate)` in `src/search/mod.rs`
  so in-tree callers can use it while the API is still gelling. Module
  exports stay internal until the next slice makes use of them.
- `src/search/readiness.rs` (new, ~16 KB):
    * `LexicalReadinessState` — `Missing`, `Repairing`,
      `StaleButSearchable`, `Ready`, `CorruptQuarantined`. Invariant:
      search is correct in `StaleButSearchable` and `Ready`,
      degrading-but-serving in `Repairing`, unavailable in `Missing`
      and `CorruptQuarantined`.
    * `SemanticReadinessState` — `Absent`, `Backfilling`,
      `FastTierReady`, `HybridReady`, `PolicyDisabled`. `Absent` and
      `PolicyDisabled` both mean "no semantic refinement" but have
      distinct operator implications (missing artifacts vs.
      intentional policy gate).
    * `SearchRefinementLevel` — what a particular completed search
      actually returned: `LexicalOnly`, `FastTierRefined`,
      `FullyHybridRefined`. Independent of tier *readiness*: a search
      can be `LexicalOnly` because semantic is backfilling, because
      policy disabled it, or because the user asked for lexical.
  Each enum carries its own `#[must_use]` constructor set, doc
  comments spelling out when each variant fires, and unit tests that
  pin the invariants.
- `#![allow(dead_code)]` at module scope because this slice lands the
  types without call sites — the follow-up slices will wire them in
  and the lint falls off. The top-of-file comment calls this out
  explicitly so it isn't mistaken for forgotten code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
